records.ety: [Cf. Icel. höss gray; akin to AS. hasu, heasu, gray; or Armor. azen, zen, warm vapor, exhalation, zephyr.]

defs: Light vapor or smoke in the air which more or less impedes vision, with little or no dampness; a lack of transparency in the air; hence, figuratively, obscurity; dimness.
quotes: O'er the sky The silvery haze of summer drawn.

defs: A state of confusion, uncertainty, or vagueness of thought or perception; as, after the explosion, people were wandering around in a haze.
To be hazy, or thick with haze.

records.ety: [Cf. Sw. haza to hamstring, fr. has hough, OD. hæssen ham.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Hazed (hāzd); p. pr. & vb. n. Hazing.]

defs: To harass by exacting unnecessary, disagreeable, or difficult work.

To harass or annoy by playing abusive or shameful tricks upon; to humiliate by practical jokes; -- used esp. of college students, as an initiation rite into a fraternity or other group; as, the sophomores hazed a freshman.
